title: cytisus decumbens var. procumbens
intro: Cytisus decumbens var. procumbens, also known as procumbent broom, is a low-growing, spreading shrub that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to the presence of glycosides and other compounds. The health benefits of Cytisus decumbens var. procumbens include its potential to help with urinary issues, such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Herbal preparations of this plant are often found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
